channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: New OXLOADER Loader Uses Malicious Google Ads to Deliver CastleStealer
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new campaign that delivers CastleStealer by means of a previously unreported malware loader dubbed OXLOADER.
According to Elastic Security Labs, ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Google Sets Sept. 30 Deadline for Android Developer Verification in Four Countries
item.description:
Google has set September 30, 2026, as the day it begins enforcing Android developer verification in the first four countries, and the major device-maker app stores are in from the start.
On that date...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Stop Your Legacy Infrastructure from Hijacking Your AI Agents
item.description: Earlier this month, I spoke at the Gartner Security & Risk Management Summit about a blind spot most security programs are still not accounting for - how attackers are circumventing AI security progra...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Browser Bugs, EDR Killers, TV Botnet, OpenBSD Flaw, Android Trojan, and More
item.description:
It’s Monday again.
This week’s threat list looks painfully familiar: abused integrations, fake tools, poisoned websites, ransomware crews trying to shut down security tools, and mobile malware asking...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Canada’s Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices
item.description:
Canada's spy service got a judge's permission to reach into infected servers, home routers, and IoT gear sitting on Canadian soil and neutralize two foreign-run botnets.
The Federal Court released a ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
item.description: A new malware family is turning forgotten home routers into a distributed reconnaissance and proxy network, not the DDoS botnet these devices usually end up in. QiAnXin's XLab calls it AryStinger and ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
item.description: A new report from INTERPOL has revealed a "dramatic increase" in cybercrime in Asia and the South Pacific, fueled by rapid digitalization, internet penetration, new technologies, organized criminal ne...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide
item.description: A previously undocumented malware botnet named AryStinger has compromised more than 4,000 outdated routers to turn them into proxies for malicious traffic. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New Prinz Eugen ransomware prioritizes recent files for encryption
item.description: A new ransomware operation named 'Prinz Eugen' prioritizes recently modified files for encryption and leaves no ransom note on the system. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft links Mastra AI supply chain attack to North Korean hackers
item.description: Microsoft has attributed a recent Mastra AI supply chain attack that compromised more than 140 npm packages to the North Korean hacking group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
item.description:
Threat actors are exploiting a recently patched security flaw impacting Gravity SMTP, a WordPress plugin that's installed on about 100,000 sites.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-4020 (CVSS sco...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
item.description: Market intelligence platform Klue has publicly confirmed a recent security incident that allowed threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, as the new "...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting an unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the WordPress plugin Gravity SMTP, active on 100,000 sites. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Unpatchable 'usbliter8' Exploit Breaks Apple A12 and A13 SecureROM Boot Chain
item.description:
Security researchers at Paradigm Shift have published a working exploit, dubbed usbliter8, that achieves arbitrary code execution inside the SecureROM of Apple's A12 and A13 chips.
That code is burne...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Gentlemen RaaS Uses GentleKiller EDR Framework Targeting 400 Security Processes
item.description: The Gentlemen ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation is actively developing and maintaining a suite of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers that it hands out to affiliates for impairing sys...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Texas govt data breach exposes over 3 million driver’s licenses
item.description: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department (TPWD) disclosed a data breach at its license system vendor that exposed personal information for more than three million individuals. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
item.description:
Microsoft researchers have detailed an exploit chain, named AutoJack, that turns an AI browsing agent into a delivery vehicle for remote code execution.
Steer the agent to load an attacker's web page...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Operation Endgame Disrupts SocGholish Servers, Cleans 14,971 WordPress Sites
item.description: Dutch law enforcement authorities, along with counterparts from Canada , Germany, and the U.S., have disrupted malicious infrastructure associated with SocGholish and cleaned up nearly 15,000 infe...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday urged Fortinet customers with FortiGate appliances to take steps to secure against ongoing malicious activity aimed at thou...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
item.description: AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical business systems, often with little oversight. Token Security breaks down why AI agents are becoming a new identit...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Stressors, AI Forcing Changes to Cybersecurity Teams
item.description: As threats proliferate and AI complicates cybersecurity, CISOs say the job is getting harder, but more companies still want cybersecurity expertise, if even on a part-time basis....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
item.description: Modern phishing attacks, including Device Code phishing, can undermine MFA protections and grant attackers access to corporate accounts without stealing passwords. This webinar explores how behavioral...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: From Assistive to Agentic: The AI Shift That's Redefining Threat Management
item.description:
Introduction
The average enterprise security team has 40 or more security tools, giving a lot of visibility into internal telemetry and asset data. But often, these tools are working in siloes, gener...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft: June 2026 Windows updates break Recycle Bin prompts
item.description: Microsoft has confirmed a confusing Windows bug that causes different filenames to appear in the confirmation dialog when deleting a file from the Recycle Bin. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA: Splunk Enterprise flaw actively exploited, patch by Sunday
item.description: CISA has urged U.S. federal agencies to secure their systems by Sunday against a critical Splunk Enterprise vulnerability that is being exploited in attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
item.description: The first wave of enterprise AI concern was straightforward. It was simply employees pasting sensitive data into public AI tools. Security teams responded with usage policies, domain blocks, and data ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
item.description:
Salesforce has revealed that it disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration within its platform in response to a security incident impacting the competitive intelligence company on June 11, 2026.
T...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: NY man charged after harassing college student with AI-generated nudes
item.description: A New York man faces cyberstalking charges after allegedly sharing AI-generated nude images and fabricated racist messages using fake social media profiles to harass a Georgia college student. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) urged Fortinet customers to secure their devices after nearly 74,000 firewall and VPN credentials were exposed in a data leak dubbed "F...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Apple Patches Beats Studio Buds Flaw Letting Nearby Attackers Spy via Microphone
item.description:
Apple has updated its Beats Studio Buds wireless earbuds to patch a high-severity vulnerability that could be exploited by nearby hackers to eavesdrop on users.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2025...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
